mainEntity.name: What commercial kitchen design services does AIM Integrated Designs offer?

acceptedAnswer.text: We design commercial kitchens that perform under daily service pressure — from high-volume hotel banquet operations to fast-casual restaurant lines. Our deliverables include equipment floor plans, elevation drawings, MEP rough-in coordination, exhaust and make-up air calculations, health department compliance packages, and equipment specification schedules.

mainEntity.name: What BIM modeling services are available?

acceptedAnswer.text: We build parametric Revit models at LOD 200–400 so every stakeholder works from one source of truth. Our BIM workflow includes clash detection routines, issue tracking dashboards, and structured data exports (COBie, IFC) that integrate with construction management platforms.

mainEntity.name: How does the floor planning process work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our process starts with programming interviews and benchmarking — understanding seat counts, throughput targets, storage needs, and ADA requirements. Test-fit studies let clients evaluate multiple layout options with real square-footage data and cost implications before committing to a direction.

mainEntity.name: What is AIM Integrated Designs' project process?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our process follows six disciplined phases: Discovery & Brief, Programming & Analysis, Concept Design, Design Development, Documentation & Coordination, and Construction Support. Each phase keeps scope, schedule, and budget aligned from first conversation to final handoff.

servicesAccordion.title: A closer look at each discipline.
servicesAccordion.subtitle: Every service is structured around buildable outcomes — not abstract design theory.

items.title: Commercial Kitchen Design
items.summary: End-to-end kitchen systems engineered for real-world service pressure.
details: We design commercial kitchens that perform under the intensity of daily service — from high-volume hotel banquet operations to fast-casual restaurant lines. Every layout begins with workflow analysis: how food moves from receiving through prep, cook line, plating, and service.

Our deliverables include equipment floor plans, elevation drawings, MEP rough-in coordination, exhaust and make-up air calculations, health department compliance packages, and equipment specification schedules. We coordinate directly with mechanical engineers to ensure hood systems, grease traps, and utility connections are resolved before construction begins.
Whether you are opening a single restaurant or rolling out a multi-unit franchise prototype, our kitchen packages are built so general contractors can price, permit, and build without ambiguity.

items.title: BIM Modeling & Coordination
items.summary: Authoritative 3D models that keep every discipline aligned from design through construction.
details: Building Information Modeling is the backbone of our coordination process. We build parametric Revit models at the appropriate Level of Development (LOD 200–400) so every stakeholder — architects, structural engineers, MEP consultants, and trades — works from one source of truth.

Our BIM workflow includes clash detection routines, issue tracking dashboards, and structured data exports (COBie, IFC) that integrate with construction management platforms. We run coordination meetings with marked-up 3D views so conflicts are resolved in the model, not on the job site.

For kitchen and food service projects, we model equipment in 3D with connection points, clearance zones, and utility stubs so the mechanical, electrical, and plumbing trades can coordinate duct routing, drain lines, and power drops before rough-in begins.

items.title: Floor Planning & Spatial Programming
items.summary: Data-informed space planning that maximizes efficiency without sacrificing user experience.
details: Good floor planning is invisible — occupants move through a space intuitively because adjacencies, circulation paths, and functional zones were carefully resolved. We approach every floor plan as an operational diagram first: who moves where, how often, and what do they need at each point.

Our process starts with programming interviews and benchmarking — understanding seat counts, throughput targets, storage needs, and ADA requirements before a single line is drawn. Test-fit studies let clients evaluate multiple layout options with real square-footage data and cost implications.

From open-plan offices with hoteling zones to restaurant dining rooms with kitchen adjacency constraints, we deliver dimensioned plans, furniture layouts, and circulation diagrams that give interior designers and architects a solid operational foundation.

items.title: Architectural Design & Documentation
items.summary: Design intent carried from concept sketches through construction-ready documentation.
details: Architecture is where vision meets buildability. We carry projects from early massing studies and concept imagery through schematic design, design development, and construction documents — ensuring the quiet details that define a building's character survive value engineering and contractor interpretation.

Our documentation packages include site plans, floor plans, building sections, exterior elevations, wall sections, enlarged plans, door and window schedules, finish schedules, and specification references. We coordinate with structural, MEP, and civil consultants to produce a coordinated set that minimizes RFIs during construction.

Whether the project is a boutique hospitality interior, a ground-up mixed-use building, or a residential renovation, we maintain a cohesive architectural language — balancing material honesty, proportion, and environmental performance with the practical demands of permitting, budget, and schedule.

featuredProjects.title: Projects that define our practice.
featuredProjects.subtitle: Each project represents a unique challenge solved through integrated design thinking.

items.title: The Grand Meridian — Flagship Hotel Kitchen
items.description: A 12,000 sq ft production kitchen serving three restaurant concepts, a banquet hall, and 24-hour room service. We designed the workflow for 800+ covers per service with separate prep, cook, pastry, and garde manger stations.

items.title: Coastal Breeze — Restaurant Prototype
items.description: A scalable fast-casual restaurant prototype designed for franchise rollout. The kitchen footprint was optimized to 1,800 sq ft with a throughput target of 200 covers per hour during peak service.

items.title: Hillcrest Residence — Courtyard Wing
items.description: A 4,200 sq ft courtyard addition to an existing mid-century residence. The design centered on framing garden views from every room while maintaining thermal performance through high-performance glazing systems.

items.title: Nexus Tower — Corporate Amenity Level
items.description: Programming and space planning for a 22,000 sq ft amenity floor featuring a café, fitness center, lounge, and conferencing suite. Test-fit studies explored four layout options before the client selected the final configuration.

items.title: Harbor House — Boutique Hotel F&B Core
items.description: Full BIM coordination for a boutique hotel's food and beverage core — including a rooftop bar, ground-floor brasserie, and basement prep kitchen. LOD 350 models enabled clash-free MEP routing in a tight floor-to-floor envelope.

items.title: Union Yards — Mixed-Use Podium
items.description: A five-story mixed-use podium with ground-floor retail, two levels of structured parking, and two floors of co-working space. The architectural language bridges industrial context with contemporary materiality.

process.title: A proven path to project success.
process.subtitle: Six disciplined phases that keep scope, schedule, and budget aligned from first conversation to final handoff.

steps.title: Discovery & Brief
steps.description: We listen before we draw. Initial conversations establish project goals, operational requirements, budget parameters, and schedule constraints. The output is a clear project brief that every team member can reference.

steps.title: Programming & Analysis
steps.description: Space needs are quantified through benchmarking, adjacency diagrams, and code analysis. For kitchen projects, this includes menu-driven equipment lists and throughput calculations.

steps.title: Concept Design
steps.description: Multiple design directions are explored with floor plans, 3D massing studies, and material palettes. Clients review options with enough visual clarity to make confident decisions.

steps.title: Design Development
steps.description: The selected concept is refined with detailed drawings, equipment specifications, and BIM models. Coordination with consultants begins, and the design is tested against budget and code.

steps.title: Documentation & Coordination
steps.description: Construction documents are produced with full consultant coordination, clash detection, and specification writing. The package is assembled for permitting and contractor pricing.

steps.title: Construction Support
steps.description: We stay engaged through construction — responding to RFIs, reviewing submittals, conducting site visits, and verifying that design intent is maintained through the final punch list.

ctaBanner.title: Let's turn your vision into buildable reality.
ctaBanner.description: Whether you are launching a new restaurant, coordinating a complex BIM model, or designing a building from the ground up — we bring the same discipline to every project.
